summary: Security hardening tests complete. All 34 backend security tests passed. All protected endpoints now require JWT authentication. Dashboard password moved from client-side to server-side verification. Frontend login flows verified working.
critical_code_review_comments: Security implementation is correct - all protected endpoints now require JWT via Depends(get_current_user)
Dashboard password correctly moved from client-side JS to server-side verification via /api/dashboard/login
No hardcoded password visible in LiveDashboard.js source code
Public endpoints correctly accessible without auth: /api/health, /api/, /api/takip/{token}, /api/users/login, /api/management/login, /api/dashboard/login, /api/drivers/login, /api/visitors/log
Router-level auth dependencies properly applied to shifts, defects, analytics, warehouse, paints, ai, messages, operators, pallets, misc routes
context_for_next_testing_agent: Security hardening validated. All backend API endpoints now require JWT authentication except public endpoints. Dashboard password verification moved to server-side. Key security tests: 1) Protected endpoints (machines, jobs, dashboard/live, analytics, paints, shifts, defects, audit-logs, vehicles, drivers, visitors/stats, pallets) return 401 without token. 2) Public endpoints (health, root, login endpoints, takip, visitors/log, drivers/login) work without token. 3) All login flows return valid JWT tokens. 4) Dashboard uses sessionStorage for its token, separate from other pages' localStorage.
